name: Potato-Gruyere Gratin
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon fresh thyme leaves or 1 teaspoon dried, chopped
2 tablespoons butter or margarine, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F.
2. Grease a 3-quart gratin dish or other shallow baking dish.
3. Spread half the potato slices in the dish.
4. Sprinkle with minced garlic and half the thyme, salt, and pepper.
5. Sprinkle with Gruyere cheese.
6. Top with remaining potato slices, overlapping for an attractive top.
7. Pour chicken broth over potatoes.
8. Sprinkle remaining thyme, salt, pepper, and Gruyere cheese on top.
9. Dot with butter.
10. Bake for 30 minutes.
11. Tilt the pan to spoon up broth and baste the potatoes.
12. Continue baking for another 30 minutes or until the top is browned and potatoes are tender.
13. Let stand for 10 minutes before serving.
